title: Method and apparatus for a compact and high resolution mind-view communicator
abstract: An embodiment of a mind-view communication apparatus includes a first portable unit and a second portable unit. The first portable unit includes an eyeglass frame, at least one first optical unit disposed on the eyeglass frame for capturing at least one scene image corresponding to a field of view of a user, and at least one second optical unit disposed on the eyeglass frame for capturing at least one eye image corresponding to at least a portion of at least one eye of the user. The second portable unit is in communication with the first portable unit and includes at least one processor configured for receiving the at least one scene image and the at least one eye image, determining a direction within the field of view to which the at least one eye is directed based upon the at least one eye image, and generating a subset of the at least one scene image based on the determined direction.
claims: 1. A method for generating an enhanced image using a multi-camera device having at least a first camera module and a second camera module, the method comprising: generating first image data, using the first camera module, that is reproducible as a first image, the first image having a first image resolution; generating second image data, using the second camera module, that is reproducible as a second image, the second image having a second image resolution higher than the first image resolution, the second image overlapping with the first image within an overlap area of the first image, the first camera module and the second camera module being supported on or in a common structure, the first camera module generating the first image data at the same time or at about the same time that the second camera module generates the second image data; and using a processor, at least a portion of the first image data, and at least a portion of the second image data, generating third image data that is reproducible as an enhanced first image, at least a portion of the enhanced first image not within the overlap area having a third image resolution that is higher than the first image resolution.
